items.title: Estee Lauder taps JP Morgan to finance Puig takeover bid
items.summary: MADRID: US cosmetics giant Estee Lauder has commissioned JP Morgan to structure a financing package of around five billion euros (US$5.89 billion) for its takeover bid of Spanish firm Puig, sources told Spanish newspaper Expansion. Last month, Puig and Estee Lauder said they were exploring a deal that would create the world's largest premium beauty player, with brands including Tom Ford, Carolina Herrera, Rabanne, Jean Paul Gaultier, and Clinique under one roof. Estee Lauder did not immediately reply to a Reuters' request for comment outside of US business hours.

items.title: Court orders additional 10 months’ jail for 33 men who stormed Taman Shamelin KTV after previously imposing fines
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — A High Court decision has extended the sentences of 33 men involved in an attack on a KTV outlet in Taman Shamelin, ordering them to serve an additional 10 months in prison. According to The Star, Justice Datuk Aslam Zainuddin delivered the ruling today while reviewing a prosecution appeal against a Magistrate's Court judgment from earlier in the month. The lower court had previously imposed fines ranging from RM2,000 to RM3,000 on the men.

items.title: Geopolitical tensions slash Malaysia’s West Asia trade by 30.4pc, says Matrade chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — Malaysia’s trade with West Asia fell 30.4 per cent to RM21.41 billion amid ongoing geopolitical tensions, said Datuk Seri Reezal Merican Naina Merican. The chairman of the Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation (Matrade) said exports to the region dropped 23.6 per cent to RM9.87 billion, mainly due to lower shipments of palm oil, petroleum products and electrical and electronic (E&E) goods. “While the direct impact is still limited, we are monitoring the situation closely due to potential spillover effects on global trade,” Reezal Merican told a press briefing at the Matrade headquarters here today.

items.title: 23 compounds issued in KLCC 'ulat foto' operation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has stepped up enforcement against unauthorised photography activities around KLCC, with 28 items seized and 23 compounds issued under Op Lens Series 6/2026. In the latest operation, DBKL issued four compounds and seized three sets of equipment, including mobile phones. Overall, authorities recorded 28 seizures and issued 23 compounds, involving seven non-citizens.

items.title: Flooded stretches on Jalan Ampang Hilir, Jalan Kuching snarl KL traffic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — Several major roads in the city centre were hit by flash floods today after continuous rain since morning, disrupting traffic flow. In a Facebook post, the Kuala Lumpur Control Centre (KLCCC) under Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) said the persistent rainfall had caused water to accumulate at multiple locations. Vehicles drive through floodwaters along Jalan Kuching in Kuala Lumpur after heavy rain.

items.title: UK wage growth cools by less than expected in three months to February
items.summary: LONDON: British wage growth, excluding bonuses, slowed by slightly less than expected to 3.6 per cent in annual terms in the three months to February, the Office for National Statistics said on Tuesday. Economists polled by Reuters had mostly expected regular average weekly earnings growth of 3.5 per cent, down from 3.8 per cent in the previous month. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Matrade chairman: Malaysia’s trade shrugs off global jitters, posts 9.3pc growth in March
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — Malaysia’s trade grew 9.3 per cent year-on-year to RM272.95 billion in March 2026, driven by strong demand for electrical and electronic (E&E) goods. Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation (Matrade) chairman Datuk Seri Reezal Merican Naina Merican said exports rose 8.3 per cent to RM148.75 billion, alongside gains in petroleum products and optical equipment. “For the first quarter, exports expanded 12.7 per cent to RM426.53 billion, pushing the trade surplus up 54 per cent to RM63.22 billion.

items.title: Matrade sticks to 3.0–5.0pct trade growth forecast despite geopolitical tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation (Matrade) is maintaining its 2026 export and trade growth projection at three to five per cent despite ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Matrade chairman Datuk Seri Reezal Merican Naina Merican, however, said the forecast may be revised if conditions worsen or escalate into a global recession. "We forecast that our export growth and trade growth this year will be in the region of between three and five per cent.

items.title: Cemetery couple freed after Penang High Court revises jail term to time served following accepted representation
items.summary: In allowing the criminal revision, judge Rofiah Mohamad maintained the conviction against M. Jegathesan, 58, and Halila Abu Bakar, 37, but replaced the original sentence with imprisonment from the date of arrest on March 23 until the disposal of the revision today. “However, the 12-month sentence is replaced with a term of imprisonment from the date of arrest until today. She said the agreement maintained the conviction but sought a reduction in the custodial term.

items.title: Malaysia targets 32pct installed renewable energy capacity this year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is committed to reaching 32 per cent installed renewable energy (RE) capacity this year, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said. Fadillah, who is also the Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation, said the country had achieved 31 per cent installed RE capacity as of December 2025. "The government aims to increase installed renewable energy capacity by one per cent a year until we reach 35 per cent by 2030 or earlier.

items.title: Syed Saddiq warns new prosecutor role could be among Malaysia’s most powerful, calls for checks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — The proposed law to separate the role of public prosecutor (PP) from the attorney general (AG) could result in the former being one of the most powerful persons in Malaysia, according to Muar MP Syed Saddiq Abdul Rahman. For this reason alone is why he said the power to appoint the PP should not be decided by one person, but decentralised during the selection and appointment process. But Syed Saddiq said that the AG currently comes under the Prime Minister’s Office.

items.title: Home Ministry to give input on Turap in Cabinet
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Home Ministry will provide its input on the proposed new foreign worker recruitment system in the Cabinet. The proposed system, known as the Universal Recruitment Advanced Platform (Turap), was developed by Bestinet Sdn Bhd. The proposed system would be integrated with existing platforms, including the Foreign Worker Centralised Management System (FWCMS) and the National Integrated Immigration System (NIISe).

items.title: 'I'll slaughter you all tonight': Man jailed over threat to mother
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A scrap metal collector has been jailed for seven months after pleading guilty to threatening to kill his mother and possessing stolen copper wire. Johari Ramlan, 36, entered the plea after two charges were read before magistrate Arun Noval Dass at the Yong Peng magistrate's court. On the second charge, Johari was found in possession of stolen copper wire at an abandoned house in Jalan Sungai Suluh Besar, Senggarang, at 3.20pm on April 17.

items.title: Perak struggles to attract major hotels as tourists skip overnight stays
items.summary: IPOH, April 21 — Difficulties in attracting major international hotel brands to Perak were highlighted in the State Legislative Assembly, as most tourists visiting the state prefer day trips rather than overnight stays. Perak Tourism, Industry, Investment and Corridor Development Committee chairman Loh Sze Yee said the state continues to be seen largely as a day-trip destination among travellers. Likewise, visitors from Penang often come to Perak for the day but choose to stay overnight in Kuala Lumpur instead,” he said.

items.title: KL mayor goes on the ground to assess flood and landslide damage in Segambut
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud visited several locations in the city hit by flash floods yesterday, including Bukit Lanjan, where a landslide also occurred. The affected locations in the Segambut district included Kampung Sungai Penchala, Kampung Segambut Dalam, and Jalan Seri Bintang. Yesterday's heavy rain also triggered a mud flood in Taman Tun Dr Ismail (TTDI), occurring just a day after a stop-work order was issued for the Bukit Kiara Federal Park administrative zone office project.

items.title: Fahmi extends condolences to family of veteran newsman Joseph Bingkasan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has expressed his condolences to the family of Joseph Bingkasan, the former New Straits Times (NST) editorial head for Sabah and Sarawak, who died early today. He also served at Times of Malaysia London Ltd, the NST office in London, before being appointed editorial head of NST Sabah and Sarawak, based in Kuching. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: 'Pisang goreng' seller fined RM3,500 for outraging sister-in-law's modesty
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A "Pisang Goreng" seller was fined RM3,500 by the Yong Peng Magistrate's Court today after pleading guilty to outraging the modesty of his sister-in-law two months ago. Magistrate Arun Noval Dass also ordered the 31-year-old accused to serve six months in jail if he failed to pay the fine. The accused paid the fine.

items.title: KLIA baggage handling disruption: Power supply system in good condition of day of incident, says TNB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) said its power supply system to the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) was in good condition on April 18, when the airport's Baggage Handling System (BHS) experienced a breakdown. TNB, in a statement, said no supply disruptions were recorded on the day of the incident. "The electricity supply system to KLIA was in good condition on the day of the incident.

items.title: Maybank to shift to new HQ at Menara Merdeka 118 from May 6
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malayan Banking Bhd (Maybank) says it will officially relocate its head office from Menara Maybank on Jalan Tun Perak to Menara Merdeka 118 here effective from May 6. Several subsidiaries, including Maybank Islamic Bhd and Maybank Investment Bank Bhd, will also be relocating to the new premises in a phased and staggered manner. Maybank said its new registered address will be Level 70, Menara Merdeka 118, Presint Merdeka 118, 50118 Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Scarce food, bleak futures spur Rohingya refugees to gamble with death at sea
items.summary: ROHINGYA refugee Rahila Begum spent two days adrift in the Andaman Sea this month, clinging to a wooden shard after her overcrowded boat capsized, one of the few survivors of a disaster that left 250 missing and feared dead. She was among the thousands of Rohingya Muslims who brave hunger and accidents on rickety boats each year to flee desperate conditions in camps in southeastern Bangladesh for countries such as Indonesia and Malaysia. Hundreds die en route from hunger or accidents at sea, but the numbers keep growing as shrinking food rations caused by dwindling international aid push yet more to make the dangerous crossing.

items.title: 23-year-old soldier faces child sexual assault charges
items.summary: MELAKA: A soldier has been charged in a Sessions Court in Ayer Keroh with two counts of sexual offences against two young girls, as well as possessing obscene material on his mobile phone. Private Muhammad Fudhail Fathollah Hamdan, 23, said he understood the charges when they were read out in court and claimed trial. The case was heard before judge Raja Noor Adilla Raja Mahyaldin, with the prosecution led by deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Qaiyyum Ramlan and the defence represented by SS Amritpal Singh.

items.title: Even the smallest groups must benefit, says PM Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said even the smallest groups must benefit from the RM10 billion allocation under the Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan (SJPP). He said the government-backed guarantee schemes should also cover agricultural groups, smallholders, fishermen, as well as small traders operating stalls and small restaurants. "We have asked all related facilities, including CGC (Credit Guarantee Corporation Malaysia Bhd) and others, under the plans of Bank Negara Malaysia and the Treasury Department, to also involve agricultural groups, smallholders, fishermen and small traders operating stalls and small restaurants.

items.title: 5 held over spate of robberies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested five men in connection with a spate of robberies in Kampung Sri Tambun, Durian Tunggal here. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the suspects, in their 20s and 30s, were detained in Perak on Sunday. The arrests follow an armed robbery in Kampung Sri Tambun on April 12, where the robbers assaulted their victims.

items.title: SMK Taman Selesa Jaya students bag four golds at Terengganu science fair
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: SMK Taman Selesa Jaya students clinched four gold medals and the best presenter award at the Terengganu International Science Fair 2026. The Johor school emerged as one of the standout performers in the applied science, environmental science and artificial intelligence (AI) categories, clinching four gold medals and the prestigious best presenter award. SMK Taman Selesa Jaya's M. Kirthika won a gold medal and was named best presenter.

items.title: Housewife arrested over seizure of five guns, claims weapons belong to husband
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The housewife who was arrested after police seized five firearms, including an M16 rifle, from a house in Kampung Miak yesterday, has claimed the weapons belong to her husband. General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the woman claimed she did not know about the firearms. "Also found were 57 shotgun pellets, 20 rounds of 5.56 mm ammunition, another 20 rounds of 5.56 mm ammunition, and 35 live rounds," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Malaysia not passive on sovereignty despite neutral diplomatic stance - Fadillah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia will not remain passive on issues of international sovereignty despite its neutral diplomatic stance, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said. Also present were editor-in-chief Arul Rajoo Durar Raj, deputy editor-in-chief (News Services) Mohd Shukri Ishak and acting deputy editor-in-chief (Economic News Services) Harlina Samson. Despite taking firm positions on sovereignty, Fadillah said Malaysia continues to balance its diplomatic ties, particularly in trade, to safeguard economic growth.

items.title: Hilux driver probed over dangerous Cameron Highlands overtake
items.summary: IPOH: Police have identified the driver seen repeatedly attempting a dangerous overtaking manoeuvre along the winding stretch between Simpang Pulai and Cameron Highlands. District police chief Muhammad Najib Hamzah said the 66-year-old man from Kampung Raja, Cameron Highlands, will have his statement recorded over the incident. The owner has yet to meet the investigating officer," he said.

items.title: Sultan Nazrin: Planetary health must be the central pillar of Asean security
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Planetary health must be the central pillar of Asean security as ecological breakdown is already displacing populations, disrupting livelihoods and undermining regional stability. Sultan of Perak Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah said ecological breakdown is one of the most under-acknowledged security threats of the era, even though its effects are already visible across the region. Sultan Nazrin said climate change, biodiversity loss, ocean acidification, water scarcity and soil degradation must be treated as core security threats, rather than issues separate from the security agenda.

items.title: Saifuddin: No impulse buys in Home Ministry procurements
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Home Ministry will use the Defence Services Asia (DSA) and National Security Asia (NATSEC) 2026 exhibition as a platform to evaluate new technologies and assets to strengthen enforcement agencies under its purview. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the exhibition brings together global industry players showcasing advancements in intelligence systems, radar capabilities and other security technologies. He said ministry officials attending the exhibition would evaluate products based on identified operational needs and capability gaps.

items.title: AI must strengthen social cohesion, not divide societies, says Sultan Nazrin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Artificial intelligence (AI) systems must be designed to reinforce social cohesion rather than erode it, as poorly governed technologies risk deepening divisions and accelerating conditions that could lead to conflict. The Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah, said Asean must ensure that artificial intelligence is deliberately shaped to support unity and trust within societies. The question is not whether Asean will use AI.

items.title: MTT Shipping dismisses Melaka Straits disruption fears, cites Hormuz contrast
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MTT Shipping & Logistics Bhd (MTTSL) has dismissed concerns over potential disruptions to the Strait of Melaka, drawing a clear distinction between the regional waterway and the conflict-affected Strait of Hormuz. Speaking at the press conference of its listing ceremony here today, MTT managing director and Shipping Association of Malaysia chairman Ooi Lean Hin said the risk profiles of the two strategic maritime routes differ significantly, particularly in terms of accessibility and global trade implications. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Policeman reassigned after viral footage shows him stepping on suspect's head
items.summary: KULIM: A policeman caught on camera stepping on a suspect's head during an arrest yesterday has been reassigned to administrative duties pending an internal investigation. Kulim deputy police chief Deputy Superintendent Tengku Mohd Faisal Tengku Yeng said the Integrity and Standard Compliance Department (JIPS) has been directed to probe the incident and determine if standard operating procedures were breached. A 29-second video circulating on Facebook shows the suspect being pulled from his motorcycle.

items.title: Food vs fuel: Survey shows what Malaysians and Thais fear most as oil jitters rise
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — A new survey has found food and fuel prices topping consumer worries in Malaysia and Thailand as tensions in the Strait of Hormuz keep oil markets volatile. Food vs fuel divide In Malaysia, 35.8 per cent of respondents cited food and groceries as their top concern, compared to 24.1 per cent who pointed to fuel despite existing subsidies. In Thailand, 34.4 per cent were most worried about fuel and transport costs, while 26.8 per cent cited food prices.

items.title: King presents instruments of appointment to 14 judges
items.summary: The ceremony began with the presentation of the instruments of appointment as Federal Court Judges to Datuk Ravinthran Paramaguru and Datuk Azmi Ariffin. They are Datuk Amarjeet Singh Serjit Singh, Datuk Seri Mohd Radzi Harun, Datuk Aliza Sulaiman, Datuk Ahmad Shahrir Mohd Salleh, Dean Wayne Daly and Datuk Seri Latifah Mohd Tahar. Also receiving the instruments of appointment were State Financial Officer Datuk Zairi Mat Ali, Penang Mufti Datuk Dr Mohd Sukki Othman, Penang Police Chief Datuk Azizee Ismail and Istana Negara Religious Officer Datuk Munir Md Salleh.

items.title: Zambry: Tun Razak legacy still shapes Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tun Abdul Razak Hussein's legacy endures and remains central to Malaysia's policies and institutions, said Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir. Zambry said academia often views legacy through personality and monuments, or 'Legacy From', while Tun Razak embodied 'The Legacy For', with a focus on shaping a nation for future generations. Zambry highlighted Tun Razak's "Nation Builder" legacy through three pillars: Adaptive, Transformative and Consensus Building.

items.title: [UPDATED] Home Ministry to verify documents of Kampung Bahagia fire victims
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Home Ministry will assess the documentation status of victims of the Kampung Bahagia fire in Sandakan on a case-by-case basis, as authorities continue registration efforts following the disaster. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said more than 9,000 people were affected, with over 1,000 houses in the water village destroyed. Saifuddin said cases involving individuals without valid identification documents would be handled carefully.

items.title: [UPDATED] Probe into SOP after policeman filmed stepping on suspect during Kulim arrest
items.summary: Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said he has instructed the Kedah police chief to expedite investigations into the incident. "All enforcement actions by police are governed by established SOPs. Saifuddin said further action, including a review by the police integrity unit, would depend on the outcome of the investigation.

items.title: Govt adds RM5bil support for MSMEs amid global energy crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government will add another RM5 billion to support micro, small and medium-sized enterprises (MSMEs) affected by the global energy crisis, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. This brings the total allocation to support MSMEs affected by the Middle East conflict to RM10 billion. The additional support comes after the Finance Ministry announced a series of measures to help MSMEs, which include a RM5 billion allocation under the Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan (SJPP) on Monday.

items.title: Act now, not after harm: Perak Sultan urges early AI regulation at global forum
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — Artificial intelligence (AI), genetic engineering and other modern technologies should be regulated before they are deployed, the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah, said today. Sultan Nazrin cautioned that the cost of governing in retrospect could prove irreversible, particularly in the climate and ecological domains. Sultan Nazrin also called on Asean member states to invest urgently in AI literacy to help communities combat deepfakes, synthetic media and algorithmically amplified disinformation campaigns.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan Undang dispute MB’s account of council advice
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 21 — Four Undang in Negeri Sembilan have challenged Mentri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun’s account of a key council meeting, disputing his claim that the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) had advised the removal of the Undang of Sungei Ujong, Datuk Mubarak Dohak. In a joint statement issued today, as reported by The Star , Undang Jelebu Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad, Undang Johol Datuk Muhammed Abdullah, Undang Rembau Datuk Abdul Rahim Yasin, and Mubarak said a media statement released by the mentri besar’s office on April 17 was inaccurate. The four further expressed disagreement with another statement issued by Aminuddin on April 20, and said they supported a declaration made on April 19 regarding changes to the state’s leadership.

items.title: Bus driver in viral video remanded for four days over dangerous driving probe
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, April 21 — An express bus driver who recently went viral on social media for allegedly driving dangerously has been remanded for four days starting today to assist in investigations. The remand order, which runs until Friday, was issued by Magistrate Teoh Shu Yee at the Alor Gajah Magistrate’s Court here against the 53-year-old man. Alor Gajah police chief Supt Ahmad Abu Bakar said initial investigations revealed that the incident was believed to have occurred at around 3.50 am while the express bus was travelling from Ipoh, Perak, to Johor Bahru.

items.title: Penang teen, 13, dies after fall from Tanjung Tokong condominium, no foul play suspected
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — A 13-year-old girl died after she is believed to have fallen from the 16th floor of a condominium in Tanjung Tokong, George Town, yesterday. Northeast District Police Chief Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad said police received a report on the incident from the public at about 6.45pm. He said initial investigations found that the victim, a Malaysian, is believed to have fallen from her unit on the 16th floor before she was found unconscious on the eighth floor of the building at around 5.49pm.

items.title: ‘Show me proof’: Perlis Perikatan rejects no-confidence plot talk as assembly delay fuels rumours
items.summary: Its chairman, Datuk Seri Shahidan Kassim, told Berita Harian that ties between PAS and Bersatu within the state government remain intact, adding that PAS central leadership has decided to back the current administration until the next general election. PAS central has decided to support this government until the election. But that is not PAS’ way.

items.title: Light tremors felt after 3.4-magnitude earthquake in Kundasang
items.summary: RANAU, April 21 — A weak earthquake measuring 3.4 on the Richter scale struck Kundasang at 3.02am today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department. The quake, centred about 8km northwest of Ranau at a depth of 10km, may have caused light tremors in surrounding areas. Ranau Fire and Rescue Station chief PPgB Rantey Fred said no reports of damage or casualties have been received with conditions remaining under control following monitoring in Kundasang and Ranau.

items.title: Express bus burns out along Semporna-Kunak road, firefighters contain blaze
items.summary: SEMPORNA, April 21 — An express bus was destroyed in a fire along Jalan Semporna-Kunak in Semporna on Monday night, with no casualties reported. The Fire and Rescue Department received an emergency call at 10.49pm and arrived at the scene at 11.10pm, where a double-decker Scania K420 bus was found 100 per cent burnt. — Daily Express

items.title: MMEA weighs non-lethal weapons to strengthen maritime enforcement
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) is currently examining the use of non-lethal weapons, including electric guns, as part of a more flexible and controlled enforcement approach. Its deputy director-general (Logistics), Maritime Admiral Datuk Saiful Lizan Ibrahim, said that the approach to the use of the weapons is in line with international enforcement practices. Earlier, Saiful Lizan represented MMEA director-general Maritime Admiral Datuk Mohd Rosli Abdullah at the opening ceremony of the Defence Services Asia (DSA) Exhibition and the National Security Asia (Natsec) Exhibition 2026 here yesterday.

items.title: Livestock worth RM110,000 seized in Perak during illegal transport operation
items.summary: IPOH, April 21 — The Perak Department of Veterinary Services (JPV) foiled a bid to illegally transport 37 sheep and 27 goats during Ops Korban Tegas in Tapah on Sunday. Its director, Dr Saifullizam Abd Kadir, said the enforcement team stopped a lorry at Kilometre 323.9 of the North-South Expressway (southbound) at about 11am. He said the livestock, believed to be intended as sacrificial stock, was estimated to be worth RM30,000, while the lorry was valued at RM80,000, bringing the total seizure to RM110,000.

items.title: Kesuma says digital foreign worker system still under review, no decisions made
items.summary: In a statement today, Kesuma said it is currently reviewing and refining the proposal through preliminary engagement sessions with labour source countries and industry stakeholders, including the technology provider of the Foreign Worker Centralised Management System (FWCMS), which has been used to manage foreign workers entering Malaysia since 2015. Taking note of various media reports and public discussions on the proposal, Kesuma said a comprehensive study of the foreign worker ecosystem found gaps in recruitment components in source countries, particularly involving debt bondage issues that could expose workers to exploitation risks. In light of this, Kesuma said it is exploring the development of a digital foreign worker recruitment system based on artificial intelligence technology to improve the ecosystem as a whole.

items.title: Mindef hands over LoA to Global Exhibitions & Conferences to promote LIMA 2027
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — The Ministry of Defence (Mindef) yesterday handed over the letter of acceptance (LoA) to Global Exhibitions & Conferences Sdn Bhd (GEC) to promote the Langkawi International Maritime and Aerospace Exhibition 2027 (LIMA 2027). It said in a statement that the LOA was handed over by Defence Ministry secretary-general Datuk Lokman Hakim Ali to GEC chief executive officer Abd Hafiz A Bakar. The handover was witnessed by the Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin after the launch of Mindef’s Pavilion at the Defence Services Asia (DSA) Exhibition and the National Security Asia (Natsec) Exhibition 2026 here yesterday.

items.title: Sarawak’s new Lawas Airport project on track for 2028 completion, says development authority
items.summary: LAWAS, April 21 — Construction of the new Lawas Airport is progressing steadily, with work currently advancing on two major components: the 4km airport access road, and Phase 1 of the airport civil works. According to the Regional Corridor Development Authority (Recoda), a progress meeting and site visit were held on April 17 to review the development of the project. Recoda said current activities at the site include clearance and grubbing works, general excavation, rock blasting, grading, as well as drainage and embankment construction.

items.title: Bus driver detained over dangerous driving caught on video, Alor Gajah police say
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, April 21 — Police have detained an express bus driver to assist investigations into a viral video showing the suspect driving in an unsafe manner recently. Alor Gajah district police chief Supt Ahmad Abu Bakar said the 53-year-old local man was arrested after he surrendered at the Alor Gajah district police headquarters Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division at about 12.10pm yesterday. He said an application for a remand order will be made at the Alor Gajah Magistrate’s Court tomorrow to facilitate further investigations.

items.title: Kota Kinabalu City Hall to act against organiser over Songkran Splash Run breaches
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 21 — Kota Kinabalu City Hall (DBKK) has reported that the ‘Songkran Sunset Splash Run’ programme failed to fully comply with the conditions set for its implementation. “DBKK stresses that all events held in the Kota Kinabalu must fully comply with approval conditions and prioritise safety, cleanliness and public well-being. Earlier, DBKK noted that the inaugural ‘Songkran Sunset Splash Run’ attracted strong turnout and contributed to a lively atmosphere in the city.

items.title: Anwar, Muslim World League chief discuss global conflicts, humanitarian concerns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim yesterday received a visit from the Secretary-General of the Muslim World League (MWL) Fadhilatus Syeikh Dr Abdul Karim Al-Essa and his delegation in conjunction with a two-day working visit to the country. In a post on Facebook, Anwar said the meeting discussed several international relations issues as well as the protection of the Muslim community across the continent which is facing the pressures of war and oppression. He said Fadhilatus Syeikh, who is also the Chairman of the Islamic Ulema’ Organisation, also stressed on MWL’s preparations in organising the World Conference of Religious Scholars and Scholars of Various Religions on June 12 in the capital.

items.title: Selangor Sultan attends dinner in conjunction with state assembly opening
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 21 — The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, last night graced a dinner held in conjunction with the opening of the fourth session of the 15th Selangor State Legislative Assembly. Sultan Sharafuddin, accompanied by Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin and Raja Muda of Selangor Tengku Amir Shah, was received on arrival by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari, State Assembly Speaker Lau Weng San and State Secretary Datuk Ahmad Fadzli Ahmad Tajuddin. Lau said the Selangor State Legislative Assembly was also establishing strategic cooperation and close consultation with the Malaysian Parliament in an effort to revive the Parliamentary Services Act.

items.title: Hajiji: RM2,000 immediate aid for each household after fire leaves 9,000 homeless in Sandakan
items.summary: SANDAKAN, April 21 — The state government has channelled immediate assistance to victims of Sunday’s pre-dawn fire in Kampung Bahagia, Batu Sapi, which destroyed nearly 1,000 houses and made some 9,000 people homeless. As initial assistance, each Household Head (KIR) received RM2,000 comprising RM1,000 in special aid from the State Government and RM1,000 from Yayasan Kebajikan Sabah. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the state government deeply sympathised with the plight of those affected, which he said was one of the largest incidents involving the settlement.

items.title: Woman arrested, five firearms seized during raid targeting illegal wildlife activities in Kelantan
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 21 — Police arrested a woman and seized five firearms of various types during a raid on a house in Kampung Miak, Kuala Krai, yesterday. General Operations Force Southeast Brigade Commander SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the raid was carried out at 6.45pm under Ops Bersepadu Khazanah, involving the 8th Battalion, the Kelantan Forestry Department and the Department of Wildlife and National Parks. During the inspection, five firearms of various types were discovered,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia and Singapore welcome temporary ceasefire, urge lasting solution to Middle East conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — Malaysia and Singapore have welcomed the temporary ceasefire involving the United States, Iran, Israel and Lebanon following the West Asia conflict, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the understanding was reached through a phone conversation with Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong to discuss the latest developments in the conflict, with its impact increasingly impacting global energy and supply chains, including in this region. Malaysia also supports ongoing diplomatic efforts, including Pakistan’s role in paving the way for a dialogue.

items.title: Wisma Putra: No reports of Malaysians harmed after strong quake in Japan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — No Malaysians have been reported seriously affected, aside from minor disruptions, following a powerful magnitude 7.5 earthquake that struck off the coast of Iwate Prefecture in northern Japan on Monday afternoon. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs, through the Embassy of Malaysia in Tokyo, is closely monitoring developments following the earthquake, which occurred at 4.53pm (local time). Japanese authorities have issued tsunami warnings for coastal areas, with waves of up to three metres expected.

items.title: Flash floods hit Cameron Highlands after heavy rain, no injuries reported
items.summary: KUANTAN, April 21 — Continuous heavy rain lasting over two hours triggered flash floods in the Habu Heights area of Cameron Highlands yesterday afternoon. Cameron Highlands police chief Superintendent Azri Ramli said the incident followed a heavy downpour that began at 2pm in the hilly terrain, resulting in a swift flow of muddy water onto the Ringlet main road. “The runoff from hilly areas surged onto the main road, causing flash floods.

items.title: Decomposed body found on Pulau Angsa coast, Kuala Selangor police open murder probe
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 21 — The decomposed body of a man wrapped in canvass was found among the rocks by the shoreline at Pulau Angsa, Jeram, Kuala Selangor on Saturday. Kuala Selangor district police chief Supt Azaharudin Tajudin said they received a call from an angler at about 4.50 pm about the discovery of a suspicious looking package emitting a foul smell while they were fishing at the location. “Further checks by the police forensics team revealed that the package contained a decomposed body that was wrapped in canvass and tied with rope.
